user_input: For the past few days, there has been a painful lump in my thigh. This lump usually appears on the outer side of the thigh and increases when I lift my leg or stand up. I don’t think it is cancerous; it might have developed due to excessive stretching or overuse. Generally, it feels dull, reducing at night. No swelling or hard tissue is observed. Could this be a common muscle strain? What should I do to treat it correctly?

updated_state.additional_notes: Could this be a common muscle strain? What should I do to treat it correctly?

follow_up_questions: On a scale of 0 to 10, how would you rate the severity of your thigh pain?
How long have you been experiencing this pain in detail?
Can you describe any specific functional limitations or activities that are affected by this pain?
